events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need reason. Scenario: shackle side-loaded. In rigging, shackle should not be side-loaded; only loaded in line with bow or pin axis depending on configuration; side loading reduces WLL and can cause pin/bow failure. Severity high/critical depending on lift. Need fail. Component conditions: installed-defective? non-compliant. Work stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 50? value not asked. confidence 1.0. findings: shackle subjected to side loading, misalignment between sling leg and shackle axis, exceeds allowable loading condition, potential for shackle failure. actions: cease lift, remove load, replace/reconfigure shackle with proper orientation, ensure load aligned with shackle bow or pin per manufacturer, use spacer/adapter if needed, inspect shackle, re-brief riggers, competent person review. references: ASME B30.26 (shackles), OSHA 1926.251. Need JSON only. Ensure valid JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 1.0,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Shackle is side-loaded, with the load applied at an angle to the shackle bow/pin axis.",
    "Side loading a shackle reduces its working load limit and can cause pin deformation, bow opening, or catastrophic failure.",
    "Configuration does not comply with manufacturer and rigging standard requirements for in-line loading."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop the lift and secure the load.",
    "Remove or unload the side-loaded shackle.",
    "Re-rig so the shackle is loaded in line with its bow or pin axis per the manufacturer's intended use.",
    "Use an appropriately sized shackle and, if needed, a properly rated adapter or spacer to avoid angular loading.",
    "Inspect the shackle for deformation, wear, or damage before returning it to service; remove from service if suspect.",
    "Have a qualified/competent person review and approve the rigging configuration before resuming."
  ],
  "rationale": "ASME B30.26 and OSHA 1926.251 require shackles to be loaded in line with their intended axis; side loading is prohibited because it drastically reduces capacity and creates a risk of sudden failure during a heavy lift.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.26 - Rigging Hardware (shackles)",
    "OSHA 1926.251 - Rigging equipment for material handling"
  ]
}
